channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senator Proposes Office to Regulate AI in Kenya with Fines and Penalties
item.description: Nominated Senator Karen Nyamu has tabled a Bill aiming to create an Office of the Artificial Intelligence Commissioner in Kenya, reflecting the need for formal regulation as AI use grows across sector...

item.title: Conviction of Previn Pillay's killers concludes long legal ordeal for family
item.description: The family of 29-year-old Previn Pillay has finally received a sense of justice following the sentencing of his killers in the Western Cape High Court on 12 March. Previn was tragically murdered in De...

item.title: Daughter Rescues Mother from Life-Threatening Situation at Aligarh Station
item.description: At Aligarh railway station in Uttar Pradesh, a woman narrowly escaped a tragic accident thanks to the quick reflexes of her daughter. Reports suggest that the mother was in danger at the station platf...

item.title: DJ Mafdee Shares His Experience After Winning American Cola Club Talent Competition
item.description: After five weeks of serious competition, DJ Mafdee crowned himself the winner of the American Cola Club Talent Search. But as e dey show, winning no be the end, na just the beginning of another journe...

item.title: Congress walks out of Chhattisgarh Assembly after MNREGA adjournment motion is disallowed
item.description: On 17th March 2026, the Congress party staged a walkout in the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly after its adjournment motion seeking discussion on the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarant...

item.title: The International Duty to Protect Civilians from Human Rights Abuses in Iran
item.description: The article argues that the global community has a moral and legal obligation to intervene when governments commit crimes against humanity, focusing on Iran. It highlights the Responsibility to Protec...

item.title: Five-week hot bath regimen may enhance endurance and aerobic capacity in trained runners
item.description: For decades, elite runners have relied on high-altitude training to boost red blood cell production and improve endurance. However, this method is expensive and impractical for most recreational runne...

item.title: TIB Bank and UNDP Partner to Strengthen Financing for Sustainable Economic Growth
item.description: Dar es Salaam – TIB Development Bank don enter MoU ya four years with United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) to improve financing systems wey go support inclusive and sustainable economic growth ...

item.title: The role of artificial intelligence in accelerating modern military operations
item.description: The recent US-Israel conflict in Iran has been described as the first AI war, though artificial intelligence in military operations is part of a long-standing trend of accelerating the 'kill chain' – ...

item.title: Pakistani National Dies in UAE Due to Falling Missile Debris Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Another Pakistani citizen has tragically lost his life in the United Arab Emirates after debris from a ballistic missile interception fell in the Baniyas area, according to the Abu Dhabi Media Office....

item.title: Government Rolls Out Cash, Fuel, and Transportation Support Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced measures to help Filipinos cope with rising fuel costs. The government has prepared cash aid, fuel subsidies, and free rides to ease the financial b...

item.title: Meta Signs Five-Year Deal Worth Up to $27 Billion for AI Compute Capacity from Nebius
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has entered into a significant long-term agreement with Nebius Group NV, an Amsterdam-based AI cloud provider, to secure artificial intelligence infrastructure. The deal, valued at...

item.title: Starmer rejects Trump's request to deploy UK ships to the Middle East amid Iran conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has firmly denied claims by former US President Donald Trump that he offered to send two British aircraft carriers to the Middle East. The dispute underscores growing tensi...

item.title: EFCC Chairman Urges National Assembly to Enact Whistleblower Protection Legislation
item.description: Ola Olukoyede, di EFCC Chairman, don call on National Assembly make dem pass strong law to protect whistleblowers wey dey expose corruption for Nigeria. E make dis call on Thursday for Calabar, Cross ...

item.title: Lok Sabha revokes suspension of eight Opposition MPs, Rajya Sabha debates Rural Development Ministry affairs
item.description: On March 17, 2026, during the second phase of the Parliament Budget Session, the Lok Sabha revoked the suspension of eight Opposition MPs through a voice vote. This decision came after Congress Chief ...

item.title: Two Iranian Women’s Soccer Players Begin Training with Brisbane Roar After Receiving Asylum in Australia
item.description: Two Iranian women’s soccer players, Fatemeh Pasandideh and Atefeh Ramezanisadeh, have been seen training with the Brisbane Roar in Australia after being granted asylum amid tensions between Iran, the ...

item.title: Ayushman Bharat Sanctions ₹1.73 Lakh Crore for Hospital Treatments Benefiting Millions
item.description: As of 28th February 2026, a total of 11.69 crore hospital admissions across India, amounting to ₹1.73 lakh crore, have been sanctioned under the Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-...

item.title: Funding constraints leave many Tanzanian secondary schools without science laboratories
item.description: The article explains how limited government funding is slowing down the construction and completion of science laboratories in Tanzania’s government secondary schools, especially in rural areas such a...

item.title: Iran seeks to play World Cup 2026 matches in Mexico due to safety concerns
item.description: Iran football federation is discussing with FIFA to move its World Cup 2026 matches from the United States to Mexico because of worries about the safety of its players. Mehdi Taj, president of Iranian...

item.title: PNP-ACG advises public to watch out for online scams during Lenten season and rising costs
item.description: The Philippine National Police - Anti-Cybercrime Group (PNP-ACG) has issued a warning to the public to stay vigilant against online scams, especially as fuel, travel, and product prices continue to ri...
